description: Strategic equity holdings the VidNova Trust would hold in nationally significant Ukrainian enterprises. Equity stakes preserve Ukrainian public interest while mobilising private co-investment.

assets.name: Ukrhydroenergo
assets.description: Ukraine's largest hydropower operator — 8 HPPs, 4.8 GW installed capacity. Kakhovka rebuild and Dnipro cascade restoration are the centrepiece of energy-sector reconstruction.
assets.strategic_rationale: Controls Dnipro river cascade. Post-war electricity pricing anchor. Sovereign asset with irreplaceable grid role.
assets.source: RDNA3 energy sector; Ukrhydroenergo corporate data

assets.name: Ukrenergo TSO
assets.description: National electricity transmission system operator. Controls 23,000 km of high-voltage lines. ENTSO-E synchronisation completed March 2022.
assets.strategic_rationale: Natural monopoly backbone. ENTSO-E connection gives strategic EU integration value. Reconstruction is prerequisite for all other sector rebuilds.
assets.status: partially damaged — ongoing attacks on substations
assets.source: RDNA3 energy sector; Ukrenergo grid data 2024

assets.name: Ukrzaliznytsia (National Rail)
assets.description: State railway — 22,000 km network, critical for wartime logistics and reconstruction supply chains. Operates throughout conflict with minimal interruption.
assets.strategic_rationale: Strategic logistics backbone. EU rail gauge harmonisation (1,435 mm) project ongoing — major EU integration asset. Freight revenue model supports concessional debt.
assets.co_investor_target: EIB EU4Transport, EBRD, private rail operators
assets.source: RDNA3 transport sector; UZ corporate data 2024

assets.name: Ukrtelecom / Digital Infrastructure
assets.description: National fixed-line and fibre operator plus spectrum portfolio. Digital infrastructure is a build-back-better multiplier across all reconstruction sectors.
assets.strategic_rationale: Digital infrastructure is a prerequisite for telemedicine, e-governance, and smart energy. Ukraine's digital-government lead (Diia) makes telecoms a competitive asset.
assets.co_investor_target: EU Digital Decade programme, private telecoms operators
assets.status: partially damaged — fibre resilient; tower infrastructure targeted
assets.source: RDNA3 digital sector; USAID broadband assessment 2023

assets.name: Danube Port Cluster (Izmail, Reni, Ust-Dunaysk)
assets.description: Three Danube river ports handling grain and bulk cargo export — Ukraine's wartime lifeline after Odesa port was restricted. Combined capacity ~70 mt/year potential.
assets.strategic_rationale: Permanent grain export corridor independent of Black Sea access. EU inland waterway connection to Rotterdam. Highly strategic for post-war food security diplomacy.
assets.co_investor_target: EBRD, private port operators, grain trading firms
assets.source: RDNA3 transport sector; UN grain corridor documentation

assets.name: State Grain Elevator / Agrarian Infrastructure Network
assets.description: State grain storage, logistics, and export inspection infrastructure. Odesa and Mykolaiv terminals damaged; inland elevator network critical for post-war food security.
assets.strategic_rationale: Ukraine is the world's 3rd largest grain exporter. Agricultural infrastructure is the fastest-returning commercial asset class for reconstruction finance.
assets.co_investor_target: IFC, EBRD, private agri-traders (Bunge, Cargill, Viterra)
assets.status: partially damaged — Odesa/Mykolaiv terminals severely damaged
assets.source: RDNA3 agriculture sector; KSE grain infrastructure assessment

assets.name: Boryspil International Airport
assets.description: Ukraine's main international hub — closed since February 2022. 15 mt/year capacity. Key post-war economic connectivity asset.
assets.strategic_rationale: Post-war reopening is a visible reconstruction milestone. Concession model suitable for private co-investment with Trust minority stake to retain strategic control.
assets.co_investor_target: ADP (Paris Airports), private infrastructure funds, EBRD
assets.status: closed — intact, no significant damage reported
assets.source: RDNA3 transport sector; airport corporate data

assets.name: Energoatom (Nuclear)
assets.description: State nuclear power operator — 4 plants, 13.8 GW, ~55% of Ukraine's pre-war electricity. Zaporizhzhia NPP under Russian control since March 2022.
assets.strategic_rationale: Nuclear is Ukraine's clean baseload anchor. Post-liberation Zaporizhzhia restart is the single largest reconstruction energy asset. Trust equity supports safe-restart investment.
assets.co_investor_target: Westinghouse (already contracted), EBRD, bilateral nuclear cooperation
assets.status: Zaporizhzhia under occupation; other plants operational under duress
assets.source: RDNA3 energy sector; IAEA Ukraine mission reports 2022–2024

source_notes: Equity stakes and valuations are indicative estimates for modelling purposes, not offers or guarantees. Sources: RDNA3 (World Bank 2024), KSE Institute sector assessments, EBRD Ukraine country strategy 2023.
